## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On May 10, 1994, an incredibly massive, absolutely joyous, and profoundly historic milestone was achieved in South Africa. Nelson Mandela, having spent 27 years in a brutal apartheid prison, was officially and aggressively inaugurated as the absolute, very first Black President of South Africa. His massive, globally televised inauguration fundamentally and absolutely marked the definitive, triumphant end of the horrific era of racial segregation, ushering in a massive, deeply hopeful new era of multiracial democracy and aggressive national reconciliation.

On May 10, 1940, the British political landscape experienced a massive, historically definitive seismic shift during the absolute darkest hours of World War II. Following the catastrophic failure of the Norway campaign, Neville Chamberlain was aggressively forced to resign. Winston Churchill officially and heavily assumed the office of Prime Minister, aggressively forming a massive national coalition government. His absolute, uncompromising leadership aggressively rallied the deeply terrified British public to fiercely resist the massive, impending onslaught of Nazi Germany.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On May 10, 1857, the absolute, massive, and incredibly bloody First War of Indian Independence (the Sepoy Mutiny) officially and aggressively erupted in the British cantonment of Meerut. Indian sepoys, fiercely enraged by the deeply offensive Enfield rifle cartridges, aggressively mutinied, brutally killing their British officers and heavily marching directly toward Delhi to declare the Mughal Emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar as their absolute leader. This massive, violent uprising fundamentally shook the foundations of the British East India Company.

On May 10, 1526, the incredibly massive, historically definitive First Battle of Panipat achieved its absolute conclusion. The massive, heavily outnumbered forces of Babur aggressively routed the massive army of Ibrahim Lodi. Utilizing incredibly advanced field artillery and massive, highly disciplined cavalry tactics, Babur achieved a massive, crushing victory. This monumental triumph absolutely annihilated the Delhi Sultanate and formally, aggressively established the massive foundation of the Mughal Empire across the northern Indian subcontinent.
